content: I checked the board. Initial pending write-tests task was blocked by an API-endpoints dependency, so I'm auto-claiming an unblocked pending task instead and will work it through completion now.
Claimed pending task task_1780980816_3934 (Task 1). Starting work now; will send progress updates and complete it as soon as possible.

Update: task_1780980816_3934 was already picked up by alice. I then auto-claimed two remaining unblocked pending tasks: task_1780980819_5288 (Task 3) and task_1780980819_6993 (Task 2). I’m inspecting the repo and task artifacts now to determine the concrete work needed and will complete what I can promptly.

Progress update on task_1780980816_3934: inspected repository and current artifacts. The claimed task is generic, so I’m validating the current app/docs state to identify a concrete completion action. Found an HTTP API (`api.py`) plus `API.md`; next I’m checking whether there are missing tests or documentation fixes needed to bring this task to a clean done state.

Progress update: the newly auto-claimed generic tasks (Task 2/Task 3) don’t include implementation-specific acceptance criteria in their task files, so I inspected the repo state to identify concrete outstanding work. Current actionable repo work appears to be around the in-progress API/docs tracks: api.py and API.md exist, schema.sql is present, and a write-tests task remains blocked behind API-endpoints work. I’m going to validate the current API implementation, tighten any obvious issues, and then complete the generic tasks I claimed if no further explicit scope is defined.
